summary: Global document direction is not set for RTL interface languages.
evidence: Root layout sets `lang` but does not set `dir` on `<html>`.
userImpact: Urdu/Persian users get mixed-direction layouts and inconsistent alignment across navigation/settings.
recommendation: Compute and set `dir` server-side from UI language, and keep it in sync client-side.

summary: Client language switch updates `lang` but never updates `dir`.
evidence: `setLanguage` writes cookie + `document.documentElement.lang` only.
userImpact: Switching to RTL language does not mirror UI direction at runtime.
recommendation: Set `document.documentElement.dir` based on `isUiLanguageRtl` whenever language changes.

summary: Home and Today can both appear active on `/`.
evidence: `isActive` returns true for `/` when href is both `/` and `/today`.
userImpact: Confusing route state in primary navigation reduces orientation confidence.
recommendation: Use mutually exclusive active rules for Home vs Today.

summary: Button loading state is hardcoded and variant-insensitive.
evidence: Loading label is always `Working` and spinner colors are fixed for white backgrounds.
userImpact: Non-English users see untranslated status; contrast/readability degrades on light variants.
recommendation: Expose localized loading labels and token-driven spinner colors per variant.

summary: Dark mode depends on broad class-substring overrides with `!important`.
evidence: Compatibility bridge remaps `bg-white/*`, `hover:bg-white/*`, and `bg-black/*` selectors globally.
userImpact: Theme behavior is brittle and hard to predict; future components can regress unexpectedly.
recommendation: Replace global override bridge with tokenized component styles and targeted migrations.

summary: Routing middleware uses a deprecated Next.js file convention.
evidence: Runtime warning: `middleware` convention deprecated in Next 16 in favor of `proxy`.
userImpact: Future upgrades can break auth/routing guards with reduced lead time.
recommendation: Migrate to `proxy` convention and validate route protection parity.

summary: `Date.now()` is called during render, failing purity lint.
evidence: `react-hooks/purity` flags render-time impure function usage.
userImpact: Build quality gate fails; behavior can drift across rerenders.
recommendation: Compute now-time outside render path (precomputed value or memoized server value).

summary: Typecheck fails due incompatible `quranActiveSurahNumber` optionality.
evidence: `pnpm tsc --noEmit` exits with TS2719 mismatch in queue-builder test fixture typing.
userImpact: Type-safety baseline is red, reducing trust in regression detection.
recommendation: Align fixture type with current profile type contract and enforce via shared helper.

summary: Automated frontend audits are blocked by missing Playwright system dependency.
evidence: `chromium_headless_shell` fails to launch: `libatk-1.0.so.0` missing.
userImpact: Critical routing/mobile QA cannot run in this environment, leaving blind spots in release validation.
recommendation: Install required Playwright OS deps in dev/CI image and gate releases on audit job success.

summary: Interface-language copy contract has unused action/status keys.
evidence: `applyInterfaceLanguage` and `interfaceLanguageUpdated` are defined but not consumed by UI controls.
userImpact: Copy model and behavior diverge; users get inconsistent expectations in settings.
recommendation: Either wire explicit apply/confirmation UX or remove dead keys and simplify contract.

summary: Public routing tests still target `/pricing`, which is no longer a current public route.
evidence: `CTA_ROUTES`, CORS checks, and canonical tests reference `/pricing`.
userImpact: Core public QA coverage is stale and fails to validate the live information architecture.
recommendation: Replace `/pricing` checks with active public route set (e.g., `/compare`) and update selectors accordingly.

summary: Signed-out `/quran` auth expectation diverges from current runtime behavior.
evidence: Spec expects redirect; observed status can be 200 when Clerk gating is disabled.
userImpact: Access model is environment-sensitive and difficult to reason about across staging/prod.
recommendation: Document and enforce explicit behavior matrix for auth-enabled vs auth-disabled deployments.

summary: Mobile menu toggle lacks expanded-state semantics.
evidence: Button includes `aria-label` but no `aria-expanded` or `aria-controls`.
userImpact: Screen-reader users do not receive reliable open/closed state feedback.
recommendation: Add `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and stable target id linkage.

summary: Mobile nav drawer is not implemented as an accessible modal pattern.
evidence: Drawer is a toggled `<div>` with no role/dialog semantics, no focus trap, and no Escape handling.
userImpact: Keyboard focus can escape behind drawer and navigation context becomes ambiguous.
recommendation: Implement modal nav pattern with focus trap, Escape close, and inert background.

summary: Hero messaging is hardcoded in English and bypasses i18n copy system.
evidence: Primary badge, headline, subtitle, and CTA labels are literal strings.
userImpact: Language switcher creates inconsistent bilingual UX on first-touch conversion surface.
recommendation: Move marketing hero strings into localized copy dictionaries and use language context.

summary: Auth screen text is hardcoded in English.
evidence: Title, subtitle, fallback content, and account-link text are literals.
userImpact: Localized marketing shell drops to English at login boundary.
recommendation: Map auth strings into app UI copy and render by selected language.

summary: Auth misconfiguration state exposes internal env variable names directly.
evidence: Fallback panel prints `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` and `CLERK_SECRET_KEY` placeholders.
userImpact: In misconfigured deploys, end-user trust drops due developer-facing messaging.
recommendation: Replace with user-friendly support guidance and hide internal config names.

summary: Hero contains multiple near-duplicate primary CTAs.
evidence: Primary button plus secondary button plus tertiary text link all target similar signup/open intent.
userImpact: Choice overload weakens conversion clarity, especially on mobile.
recommendation: Consolidate to one primary and one secondary action per viewport.

summary: Onboarding welcome copy is hardcoded English.
evidence: Eyebrow/title/subtitle/about/CTA strings are literal text.
userImpact: Language-selected users hit an English-only onboarding start.
recommendation: Localize onboarding content through shared copy dictionaries.

summary: Start-point flow is fully hardcoded in English.
evidence: Headers, labels, placeholders, status pills, and helper text are literal strings.
userImpact: Core setup flow diverges from selected language and raises cognitive load.
recommendation: Extract onboarding-start strings into localized copy and reuse language provider.

summary: Onboarding start-point save path does not validate API response success.
evidence: POST response is awaited but `res.ok` is never checked before success toast + navigation.
userImpact: Users can be told setup succeeded even when persistence failed.
recommendation: Validate `res.ok`, surface actionable error feedback, and block progression on failed persistence.

summary: Surah search field lacks an explicit label.
evidence: Input relies on placeholder text only (`Search surah by number or name...`).
userImpact: Screen-reader and cognitive-access users have weaker form context.
recommendation: Add visible or sr-only label bound to input id.

summary: Local storage writes are unguarded in onboarding save flow.
evidence: `localStorage.setItem` calls execute without try/catch fallback path.
userImpact: Private browsing/storage restrictions can fail silently and desync onboarding state.
recommendation: Wrap storage writes in guarded utility and provide fallback messaging.

summary: Plan preview date loop coerces local date to UTC midnight.
evidence: `new Date(`${localDate}T00:00:00Z`)` then `setUTCDate` builds week rows.
userImpact: Timezone offsets can shift displayed day sequencing versus user expectation.
recommendation: Use local date arithmetic utilities tied to profile timezone semantics.

summary: Plan-preview error state lacks direct recovery affordance.
evidence: Error copy renders in text path but no retry button/action is offered.
userImpact: Users can stall in onboarding when transient fetch errors occur.
recommendation: Add retry action and fallback route CTA in error branch.

summary: Settings hub is only partially localized.
evidence: Most titles/descriptions are hardcoded literals while only a subset uses copy dictionaries.
userImpact: Language switch leads to mixed-language settings IA and weak trust in localization.
recommendation: Move all settings card titles/descriptions into i18n copy map.

summary: Display upsell controls are permanently locked by hardcoded feature flag.
evidence: `paidEnabled()` always returns `false`.
userImpact: Users see controls they cannot unlock, creating confusion and perceived brokenness.
recommendation: Back feature gating with real entitlement state or hide unavailable controls.

summary: Display persistence failures are swallowed silently.
evidence: POST `/api/profile/display` catch block ignores errors entirely.
userImpact: Users think preferences saved when backend persistence may have failed.
recommendation: Show explicit non-blocking save-status toast and retry path on failure.

summary: Language switch immediately refreshes route on every select change.
evidence: `onChange` calls `setLanguage` then `router.refresh()` without explicit apply step.
userImpact: Users can lose in-progress context while exploring language options.
recommendation: Provide explicit Apply action or optimistic inline update with deferred refresh.

summary: Language settings surface raw backend persistence diagnostics to users.
evidence: UI prints original error string (including schema diagnostics) in warning block.
userImpact: Technical errors increase anxiety and do not guide user recovery.
recommendation: Map backend failure classes to user-safe guidance with optional debug logging.

summary: Language-copy schema and settings UI interaction model are out of sync.
evidence: Apply/update-specific copy keys exist, but UI currently auto-applies with no explicit confirmation control.
userImpact: Users and translators see wording for flows that do not exist.
recommendation: Align copy schema with actual interaction model and remove stale keys.

summary: Distraction-free mode force-redirects users from non-core routes without context.
evidence: When enabled, effect unconditionally `router.replace('/quran/read?view=compact')` for non-whitelisted routes.
userImpact: Users can be disoriented when settings/support pages unexpectedly jump away.
recommendation: Add transition notice and intentional route handoff (toast/modal) before redirect.

summary: Today core workflow header/copy is hardcoded English.
evidence: `PageHeader` eyebrow/title/subtitle and action labels are literal strings.
userImpact: Daily execution surface does not respect selected language.
recommendation: Localize all today-flow labels and status strings via shared copy provider.

summary: Session step naming and coaching copy are hardcoded English.
evidence: `stepTitle`/`stepSummary` return literals for core memorization states.
userImpact: Highest-frequency recitation loop remains non-localized for non-English users.
recommendation: Externalize session-state labels/descriptions into language copy maps.

summary: Mode guidance text hardcodes debt threshold value in UI copy.
evidence: Catch-up message references fixed `45%` threshold directly in string literal.
userImpact: If engine thresholds change, UI explanations become inaccurate.
recommendation: Read threshold from server payload/config and interpolate dynamically.

summary: Reader mode/detail toggles are navigation links, not in-place controls.
evidence: Tracking, view, phonetic, and translation toggles are rendered as `<Link>` elements.
userImpact: Every toggle causes full navigation and potential focus/scroll loss.
recommendation: Use client-side control components with state sync and preserved viewport context.

summary: Reader preferences expose raw persistence diagnostics.
evidence: Language warning prints original backend error string to end-user.
userImpact: Technical schema details leak into UX and obscure recovery actions.
recommendation: Translate backend failures into user-facing guidance and retain raw errors in logs.

summary: Session client is a very large monolith with mixed responsibilities.
evidence: Single file currently spans ~1,372 LOC with state machine, copy, sync, and rendering tightly coupled.
userImpact: Change risk and onboarding cost for core recitation flow are high.
recommendation: Split by domain (state machine, persistence, controls, presentation) with typed module boundaries.

summary: Distraction-free reader state removes context blocks without explicit mode banner.
evidence: Hero/filter/status surfaces are conditionally hidden when mode is enabled.
userImpact: Users may not understand why controls disappear after mode switch.
recommendation: Add subtle persistent mode badge with clear exit affordance.

summary: Dashboard command-deck copy and controls are hardcoded English.
evidence: Header/title/subtitle/KPI labels are literals with no language abstraction.
userImpact: Engagement analytics surface is inconsistent with selected app language.
recommendation: Introduce localized copy map for dashboard headings, labels, and actions.

summary: Progress page still uses hardcoded English macro copy.
evidence: Motivational heading/subtitle and multiple KPI labels are literals.
userImpact: Non-English users lose comprehension in high-value reflection surface.
recommendation: Localize progress-page narrative copy and KPI captions.

summary: Streak calendar weekday labels are hardcoded English abbreviations.
evidence: Calendar header maps fixed array `[Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun]`.
userImpact: Locale/date cognition degrades for non-English users.
recommendation: Generate localized weekday labels via Intl APIs based on active UI language.

summary: Sparkline chart component is always `aria-hidden`.
evidence: SVG sets `aria-hidden="true"` while being used for trend cues in dashboard cards.
userImpact: Assistive-tech users miss trend information that informs decision-making.
recommendation: Expose accessible text summary or non-hidden semantic chart alternative.

summary: Billing plan pricing is hardcoded as USD string.
evidence: Paid plan renders literal `$7 / month` copy with no locale/currency abstraction.
userImpact: Global users may misinterpret price context and billing assumptions.
recommendation: Move plan pricing/currency to configurable billing presentation layer.

summary: Legacy command palette is marked dialog but lacks robust modal behavior.
evidence: No focus trap/inert background management accompanies `role=dialog` + `aria-modal=true`.
userImpact: Keyboard/screen-reader users can lose context or focus behind overlay.
recommendation: Implement managed dialog primitives with focus lock and return-focus semantics.

summary: Legacy mobile bottom nav ignores safe-area inset spacing.
evidence: Fixed bottom nav uses static padding without `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` handling.
userImpact: On iOS devices, controls can sit too close to or overlap system home indicator area.
recommendation: Add safe-area-aware bottom padding utilities for fixed legacy navigation.

summary: Legacy shell copy remains English-only and shortcut-centric.
evidence: UI labels like `Search everything`, `Ctrl K`, and action labels are hardcoded.
userImpact: Legacy routes are not usable in multilingual contexts.
recommendation: Localize legacy shell strings or gate legacy routes from multilingual navigation paths.

summary: Legacy nav links do not expose active route semantics.
evidence: Active styling is visual-only; links do not set `aria-current`.
userImpact: Assistive-tech users receive weaker orientation cues in legacy navigation.
recommendation: Set `aria-current=page` for active legacy nav links.

summary: Active shell wrapper re-exports legacy protected layout directly.
evidence: `src/components/shell/protected-layout.tsx` is a pass-through to `_legacy` implementation.
userImpact: Legacy assumptions continue leaking into modern architecture and increase migration drag.
recommendation: Create non-legacy protected-layout implementation and isolate legacy path dependencies.
